skill.name: global-validation
skill.description: Apply consistent validation guidance for forms, API requests, user input, business rules, and security checks. The skill emphasizes server-side validation, allowlists, type checks, input sanitization, and clear user-facing error messages.
skill.summary: Guide secure validation across forms, APIs, and external input boundaries.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a parent-directory documentation link, weak cryptography terms, and reconnaissance text. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits are false positives from prose, but the parent-directory link is a real filesystem boundary concern because the skill asks the agent to read outside the packaged skill file.

medium_findings.title: Parent Directory Documentation Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md directs the agent to read a relative path with parent-directory traversal. This is not executable code, but it can push an agent outside the packaged skill boundary and depends on an unbundled file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory sequence is present in the only referenced file and line. The context shows a documentation link, so the risk is boundary expansion rather than confirmed malicious activity.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Text Match
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 24 appear to match ordinary prose in the skill description. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing call, or encryption behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are natural-language guidance and contain no code or cryptographic operation. The file has no executable implementation where weak algorithms could be invoked.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Text Match
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance alert at SKILL.md line 16 appears in a bullet about validating business rules. No system inspection, host enumeration, command execution, or environment probing is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is business validation guidance and does not ask the agent to inspect the host system. No supporting evidence of reconnaissance exists in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal Sequence in Skill Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses a relative link that climbs multiple parent directories. This can cause agents or tooling to resolve content outside the skill package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is directly present at the cited location. The surrounding context is a documentation reference, which reduces confidence of malicious intent but confirms the risky pattern.

content.user_title: Validate Inputs Across Every Entry Point
content.value_statement: Inconsistent validation creates security gaps and confusing user feedback. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to design server-side validation with clear rules and safe input handling.
actual_capabilities: Guides validation design for forms, API requests, and external input.
Emphasizes server-side validation as the security source of truth.
Promotes allowlists, type checks, format checks, and range checks.
Includes guidance for sanitizing input against injection risks.
Supports consistent validation error messages for users.
Covers business rule validation such as balances and valid dates.
limitations: The packaged skill only contains a pointer to external validation guidance.
It does not include runnable validation libraries or code generators.
It cannot verify runtime data flows without project-specific code review.
The referenced guidance file is outside the skill package.

use_cases.title: Secure API Request Validation
use_cases.description: Define required fields, types, formats, and business rules before accepting request payloads.

use_cases.title: Consistent Form Validation
use_cases.description: Align client-side feedback with server-side validation for safer and clearer form handling.

use_cases.title: Validation Security Review
use_cases.description: Review user input paths for missing allowlists, weak sanitization, and inconsistent error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Form Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single form.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to add validation for this form. Check required fields, types, formats, and clear error messages.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an API Payload
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to design server-side validation for this API payload. Include allowlists, range checks, and rejection rules.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a security-focused review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to review this validation code. Identify missing checks, inconsistent errors, and injection risks.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Validation Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a full validation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-validation skill to create a validation strategy for forms, APIs, and business rules across this project.

output_examples.input: A checkout endpoint accepts item IDs, quantities, shipping data, and payment metadata.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a validation plan covering required fields, allowed item identifiers, quantity ranges, address format checks, and safe error messages.

output_examples.input: A profile form has client-side checks but weak server-side validation.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies server-side validation gaps and recommends matching backend rules for names, emails, dates, and optional fields.

output_examples.input: A team wants to review validation before releasing a new API.
output_examples.output: The assistant produces a checklist for input boundaries, allowlists, sanitization needs, business rules, and consistent failure responses.

best_practices: Treat server-side validation as mandatory for every external input path.
Use allowlists, strict types, and explicit ranges before sanitization.
Keep validation errors clear without exposing sensitive implementation details.
anti_patterns: Relying only on client-side validation for security decisions.
Accepting free-form input where a strict allowlist is possible.
Returning raw parser, database, or stack errors to users.

faq.question: Does this skill replace validation libraries?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for using validation patterns and libraries safely.

faq.question: Should validation happen on the client or server?
faq.answer: Use client-side validation for feedback, but enforce security and data integrity on the server.

faq.question: Can it help with API request validation?
faq.answer: Yes. It is suited for checking payload fields, formats, ranges, and business rules.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is guidance-focused and does not package executable validation code.

faq.question: Why is sanitization not enough?
faq.answer: Sanitization reduces risk, but validation should first reject data that does not meet strict rules.

faq.question: What is the main audit concern?
faq.answer: The skill references guidance outside its package through a parent-directory path.
